remedies.remedy_desc: Green tea helps with oily skin because Green tea helps with oxydenation and inflammation, making it an excellent option for managing oily skin. The tea is rich in antioxidants called catechins, which reduce sebum production and prevent the clogging of pores. Furthermore, the anti-inflammatory properties of green tea soothe irritated skin and alleviate redness caused by excessive oiliness. Additionally, green tea's antibacterial properties help control the growth of acne-causing bacteria, leading to clearer and healthier-looking skin. Lastly, green tea can be applied topically as a toner or a mask, providing a gentle and natural alternative to harsher chemical treatments for oily skin.
remedy_recipe: In a non-metal teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of high-quality green tea leaves with 8 ounces of filtered water that has been brought to a boil, then let it cool down to around 160-170°F (71-77°C); steep for 2-3 minutes to extract the antioxidants and create a soothing green tea brew.
In a separate bowl, whisk 1 egg white until frothy, then gently fold in 1 tablespoon of the cooled green tea without over-mixing, creating a nourishing and gentle face mask.
Cleanse your face with a mild cleanser, then apply the green tea and egg white mixture evenly onto your skin, avoiding the eye area, to provide deep pore cleansing, oil control, and mild exfoliation.
Relax for 10-15 minutes, allowing the green tea and egg white mask to dry on your skin, drawing out impurities, tightening pores, and reducing oiliness.

Rinse off the mask with warm water, then splash your face with cold water to close the pores, and gently pat your skin dry with a clean towel, leaving you with refreshed, matte, and revitalized oily skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Black tea helps with oily skin because it contains tannins and antioxidants that can help reduce oil production and soothe inflammation. The astringent properties of black tea help to shrink pores and tighten the skin, which can reduce the amount of oil that is released. Additionally, black tea has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help to reduce redness, swelling, and the risk of acne breakouts. Drinking black tea or using it as a toner can also help to balance the skin's pH levels, which can help to regulate oil production and promote healthy skin. Furthermore, black tea is a natural source of caffeine, which can help to improve blood flow and promote the growth of new skin cells, leading to a clearer and more even complexion.
remedy_recipe: Heat 1 cup of water to boiling, then steep 1 teaspoon of high-quality loose leaf black tea, such as Assam or Darjeeling, for 5 minutes; allow the tea to cool, then transfer to a spray bottle and store in the refrigerator.
In a small bowl, mix 1 tablespoon of brewed black tea with 1 tablespoon of honey and 1 tablespoon of apple cider vinegar until a smooth paste forms.
Cleanse oily skin thoroughly, then apply the paste to your face, avoiding the eye area; let it sit for 15-20 minutes.
Rinse off the mask with warm water, then splash your face with cold water to close the pores.
Gently pat your skin dry with a clean towel and apply a toner and moisturizer suitable for oily skin. Repeat this process 1-2 times a week for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with oily skin because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of oily skin. The tea's antiseptic and healing properties can also help soothe irritation and inflammation caused by excess oil, while its mild astringent properties can help tighten pores and give the skin a matte finish. Furthermore, chamomile tea has anti-bacterial properties that can help prevent acne breakouts caused by oily skin. Drinking chamomile tea can also help reduce stress and hormonal imbalances that can contribute to oily skin. Overall, incorporating chamomile tea into your skincare routine can help balance your skin's oil production, reduce inflammation, and promote a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_recipe: To make chamomile tea for oily skin, start by boiling 2 cups of water and adding 2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers; let it simmer for 10 minutes to allow the nutrients to infuse.
After 10 minutes, strain the tea to remove the chamomile flowers and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Optionally, add a squeeze of half a lemon for its astringent properties, which can help balance the skin's oil production.
To use the chamomile tea as a toner, soak a cotton pad in the tea and gently sweep it over your face, avoiding the eye area.

Let the chamomile tea absorb into your skin for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it off with warm water and patting your face dry with a clean towel. Use this toner once a day, ideally in the evening, for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with oily skin because Peppermint tea aids in controlling oily skin due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help regulate the production of sebum, the oily substance produced by the skin's sebaceous glands. The menthol present in peppermint tea has a cooling effect on the skin, reducing inflammation and providing relief to irritated skin. Additionally, peppermint tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier-looking skin. Furthermore, drinking peppermint tea can help reduce stress, which is a common trigger for increased sebum production, leading to oilier skin. Lastly, peppermint tea can be used topically as a toner or compress, providing a gentle, natural alternative to chemical-laden products that can strip the skin of its natural oils and exacerbate the problem of oily skin.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of fresh peppermint leaves, then let it steep for 15-20 minutes to allow the oils from the peppermint leaves to release into the water.
After steeping, strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature to avoid damaging skin.
Wash your face with a gentle cleanser, then soak a clean washcloth in the peppermint tea and place it on your face for 5-10 minutes.
Rinse your face with cool water, then pat it dry with a soft towel.
Apply a light, non-comedogenic moisturizer to lock in hydration and prevent dryness. Enjoy your refreshed, oil-balanced skin!

remedies.remedy_desc: Spearmint tea helps with oily skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce excess oil production and soothe irritation. The menthol present in spearmint tea has a cooling effect on the skin, which can help regulate sebum production and unclog pores. Additionally, spearmint tea is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier-looking skin. Drinking spearmint tea regularly may also help balance hormones that can contribute to oily skin, such as androgens. However, it is important to note that while spearmint tea can be beneficial for oily skin, it should not be used as a substitute for a consistent skincare routine and medical treatment if necessary.
remedy_recipe: To make spearmint tea for oily skin, start by boiling 8 cups of water in a large pot, then add 1 cup of fresh spearmint leaves (or 1/2 cup of dried spearmint) and let it simmer for 15 minutes to allow the nutrients to infuse.
While the tea is steeping, rinse out a clean 1-quart glass jar and its lid with hot water to ensure they are sterilized; set aside.
After 15 minutes, strain the spearmint tea through a fine-mesh sieve into the sterilized glass jar, discarding the used leaves; allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.

To use, wet a clean washcloth or cotton pad with the spearmint tea and apply it as a gentle toner to freshly cleansed oily skin, avoiding the eye area; let the tea cool down in the refrigerator for a refreshing, anti-inflammatory treatment.
Store the spearmint tea in the refrigerator for up to 1 week; shake or stir before each use, and enjoy the calming, oil-balancing benefits of this natural remedy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with oily skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help regulate sebum production and reduce skin redness and irritation. The tea also has antimicrobial qualities that can help prevent acne breakouts caused by bacteria that thrive in oily environments. Additionally, yarrow tea contains flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to improve skin health and reduce excess oiliness. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a toner can help maintain the skin's natural pH balance, preventing the overproduction of sebum that leads to oily skin. Overall, yarrow tea offers a natural and holistic approach to managing oily skin, providing a range of benefits that can support overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: To make yarrow tea for oily skin, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves; let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
After simmering, strain the tea to remove the yarrow herbs, then let it cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Optionally, squeeze half a lemon into the yarrow tea for its astringent properties, which can help reduce oiliness.
To use the yarrow tea as a toner, soak a clean cotton pad in the tea and gently dab it onto your face, avoiding the eye area.
Store any remaining yarrow tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, and use it as part of your daily skincare routine, preferably in the morning and/or evening.

remedies.remedy_desc: Nettle tea helps with oily skin because it has astringent properties that can help regulate the production of sebum, the oily substance produced by the skin's sebaceous glands. This makes nettle tea an effective natural remedy for reducing excess oil and preventing clogged pores, which can lead to breakouts and acne. Additionally, nettle tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Furthermore, nettle tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C, vitamin E, and iron, which are essential for maintaining healthy skin and promoting collagen production. Drinking nettle tea regularly can also help support the body's natural detoxification processes, which can improve overall skin health and reduce the appearance of oily skin. Overall, incorporating nettle tea into your skincare routine can provide a natural and holistic approach to managing oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried nettle leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-10 minutes; allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, and discard them; you should be left with only the liquid.
Clean your face with warm water and pat it dry using a clean towel.
Soak a cotton ball in the cooled nettle tea and apply it to your face, focusing on oily areas such as the T-zone.
Let the nettle tea absorb into your skin for 10-15 minutes, then rinse off with cool water and pat your face dry.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula tea helps with oily skin because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil on the skin. The tea's gentle astringent qualities help to tighten pores, preventing the buildup of dirt and oil that can lead to acne and other skin issues. Furthermore, calendula tea has antimicrobial properties that fight bacteria responsible for causing skin infections, promoting clearer and healthier skin. Drinking calendula tea can also support the body's internal balance, which may contribute to overall skin health and reduced oil production. Additionally, using calendula tea as a toner or compress can soothe irritated skin and reduce redness, providing a more even skin tone.
remedy_recipe: To make calendula tea for oily skin, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of dried calendula flowers; let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
While the tea is steeping, prepare a clean infuser or tea strainer and a heat-resistant container.
After 10-15 minutes, remove the tea from heat and carefully pour it through the infuser into the container, ensuring all the beneficial compounds from the calendula flowers are extracted.

Allow the calendula tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before using it as a toner or rinse for oily skin, either by soaking a cotton pad and gently patting it on your face or pouring it directly onto your skin.
For best results, use the calendula tea consistently, storing any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Burdock tea helps with oily skin because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help regulate sebum production, reducing the likelihood of acne breakouts. The tea is rich in antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting skin health and reducing excess oil. Burdock tea also contains essential nutrients such as vitamin A, B, and E, which are known to improve skin tone and reduce the appearance of oily skin. Furthermore, burdock tea has diuretic properties that can help eliminate toxins from the body, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin. Lastly, burdock tea has been used traditionally as a blood purifier, which can help improve overall skin health and reduce the symptoms of oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Bring 4 cups of water to a boil, then add 2 tablespoons of dried burdock root, reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
While the tea is steeping, prepare a clean tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Pour the burdock tea through the infuser or strainer into a teapot or heat-resistant container.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature, then gently rinse your face with the tea using a clean washcloth or your hands.
Let the tea soak into your skin for about 10 minutes, then rinse off with warm water and pat your face dry.

remedies.remedy_desc: Red clover tea helps with oily skin because Red clover tea helps with oxydenezenze production, which can reduce excess oil on the skin. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritation and redness caused by oily skin. Additionally, red clover tea contains isoflavones, which can help regulate sebum production, preventing the skin from becoming too oily. The tea also has a gentle exfoliating effect, removing dead skin cells and unclogging pores, which can help reduce the appearance of oily skin. Furthermore, red clover tea has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help prevent acne breakouts that are often associated with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: To make red clover tea for oily skin, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of dried red clover flowers; let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
While the tea is steeping, prepare a infusion by combining 1/4 cup of boiling water with 1 tablespoon of crushed red clover leaves; let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
After both the tea and infusion have finished steeping, strain the liquid from both mixtures and discard the solids.
Combine the strained red clover tea and infusion, then let the mixture cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
To use, drink 1-2 cups of the red clover tea daily, or use it as a toner by allowing it to cool completely and applying it to your skin with a cotton ball.

remedies.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with oily skin because it has natural detoxifying properties that can help eliminate toxins and reduce excess oil production in the skin. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation, resulting in a more balanced and clear complexion. Dandelion tea also contains essential vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and zinc, which are important for maintaining healthy skin and reducing oiliness. Furthermore, the tea has diuretic properties, which can help flush out excess fluids and toxins from the body, leading to a decrease in oiliness and improved skin health. Finally, dandelion tea has anti-microbial properties that can help prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria, making it a great natural remedy for oily and acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: To make dandelion tea for oily skin, start by boiling 2 cups of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of dried dandelion flowers; let it simmer for 10 minutes to allow the nutrients to steep.
While the tea is brewing, prepare a small pot of chamomile tea, using 1 teaspoon of chamomile flowers per cup of water, and steep for 5 minutes.
After both teas are ready, mix equal parts of dandelion and chamomile teas in a cup, then let the mixture cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Apply the cooled tea mixture to your face using a clean cotton pad, gently patting it onto your skin and avoiding the eye area.
Leave the tea on your skin for 15-20 minutes, then rinse off with lukewarm water and pat your face dry with a soft towel. Repeat this process 2-3 times a week for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tea helps with oily skin because it contains antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can regulate the production of sebum, a natural oil produced by the skin. By reducing sebum production, milk thistle tea can help prevent clogged pores and acne breakouts, which are common issues for those with oily skin. Additionally, milk thistle tea has been shown to have anti-aging effects, which can help reduce the appearance of large pores and improve the overall texture and tone of the skin. Furthermore, milk thistle tea may help soothe irritation and redness, providing relief for those who experience skin flare-ups or sensitivity. Finally, milk thistle tea is a natural and gentle alternative to harsher chemical treatments for oily skin, making it a safe and effective option for long-term use.
remedy_recipe: In a sterilized teapot, add 1 teaspoon of dried milk thistle flowers and leaves to 8 ounces of boiling water, then let it steep for 10-15 minutes with a lid to ensure the beneficial compounds from the plant are fully extracted.
After steeping, strain the tea into a glass or ceramic cup using a fine mesh strainer to remove any residual plant material.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Optionally, squeeze a lemon wedge into the tea for added vitamin C and a refreshing taste.
Drink the tea twice a day, once in the morning and once in the evening, for at least a month to see improvements in your skin's oil production and overall complexion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea tea helps with oily skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, which is often a concern for people with oily skin. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral effects, which can help to combat the bacteria that can lead to breakouts. Furthermore, echinacea is rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to tighten pores and reduce the appearance of oily skin. The tea also contains antioxidants that can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to aging and skin problems. Finally, drinking echinacea tea can help to boost the immune system, which can lead to overall healthier skin.
remedy_recipe: To make echinacea tea for oily skin, start by boiling 2 cups of water and adding 2 teaspoons of dried echinacea flowers; let it simmer for 15 minutes.
After 15 minutes, remove the tea from heat and let it cool down for about 5 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the echinacea flowers, then add the juice of half a lemon and 1-2 teaspoons of honey to sweeten.
Mix the tea well, ensuring that the honey is fully dissolved.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 1-2 times a day for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Red raspberry leaf tea helps with oily skin because it has astringent properties that can help regulate the production of sebum, the natural oil produced by the skin. This tea also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne and other skin conditions. Additionally, red raspberry leaf tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily skin and other skin problems. Furthermore, this tea has anti-microbial properties that can help prevent the growth of bacteria that can lead to acne and other skin infections. Finally, drinking red raspberry leaf tea can help support overall skin health by promoting circulation and providing the body with essential nutrients that are needed for healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan or kettle.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried red raspberry leaves to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the leaves and let them steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea leaves and let the tea cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea daily, or use it as a toner by allowing it to cool completely and applying it to your skin with a cotton ball.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with oily skin because it contains antibacterial and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation. These properties can help unclog pores, prevent acne breakouts, and soothe irritated skin. Additionally, sage is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote skin cell regeneration. Drinking sage tea regularly can also help improve digestion and reduce stress, both of which can contribute to oily skin. However, it's important to note that while sage tea can be beneficial for oily skin, it should not be used as a substitute for a consistent skincare routine and medical treatment if necessary.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, bring 1 cup of water to a boil, then add 1 teaspoon of dried sage leaves, reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes to create the sage tea base.
While waiting, cleanse your face with a gentle cleanser and pat it dry to prepare your skin for the sage tea.
After simmering, strain the sage tea to remove the leaves and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature, usually around 5-10 minutes.
Soak a clean washcloth in the warm sage tea, then gently wring it out and place it over your face for 5-10 minutes to open up your pores and allow the sage's natural oils to be absorbed.
Finally, remove the washcloth and splash your face with cool water to close the pores, then apply a light, oil-free moisturizer to lock in the benefits of the sage tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tea helps with oily skin because it contains berberine, an alkaloid known for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help regulate sebum production and soothe irritation caused by excessive oiliness. The tea also has astringent properties that can help tighten pores and reduce the appearance of shine, providing a matte finish to the skin. Additionally, goldenseal tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from environmental stressors that can exacerbate oil production, while its anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce redness and calm inflamed skin. By promoting a healthy skin microbiome, goldenseal tea can also help balance the bacteria on the skin, reducing the overproduction of oil and promoting a clearer, more even complexion. Overall, goldenseal tea can be a valuable addition to a skincare routine for those with oily skin, providing a range of benefits that can help manage oil production and promote a clearer, healthier-looking complexion.
remedy_recipe: In a sterilized teapot, add 1 teaspoon of dried goldenseal root to 8 ounces of boiling water, then let it steep for 15-20 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds and achieve a strong infusion.
After steeping, strain the tea to remove goldenseal root particles, ensuring a smooth and enjoyable drinking experience.
Allow the goldenseal tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature, ideally around body temperature, to prevent any potential skin irritation from hot liquids.
Dip a clean cotton ball or reusable cloth pad into the cooled goldenseal tea, allowing it to soak up the tea without dripping.

Gently apply the soaked cotton ball or cloth pad to your oily skin, focusing on problem areas, and leave it on for 5-10 minutes to let the tea's natural astringent properties work, then rinse off with lukewarm water.

remedies.remedy_desc: Oregon grape tea helps with oily skin because it contains berberine, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and sebum-regulating properties. This means that it can help reduce inflammation and oil production in the skin, which can contribute to acne and other skin issues. Additionally, Oregon grape tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help to kill bacteria on the skin that can lead to breakouts. The tea also has astringent qualities, which can help to tighten pores and reduce the appearance of oily skin. Furthermore, Oregon grape tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: To make Oregon grape tea for oily skin, start by bringing 2 cups of water to a boil, then add 2 teaspoons of dried Oregon grape root and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
While the tea is steeping, prepare a strong infusion of chamomile by steeping 2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers in a separate cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
After both infusions have cooled, combine them in a glass pitcher, then add 4 cups of cold water and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice to help balance the pH of the tea.
Before each use, strain a portion of the tea and mix it with 1/2 cup of cool water in a glass; refrigerate this mixture for at least 30 minutes to chill it before applying it to your skin.

To use, soak a clean cotton cloth in the chilled tea and apply it to your face for 10-15 minutes, then rinse your face with cool water and pat it dry. Store any remaining tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Licorice root tea helps with oily skin because it contains active compounds like glycyrrhizic acid and flavonoids, which have been shown to regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation. These compounds work by inhibiting the 5-alpha-reductase enzyme, which is involved in sebum production, and by reducing the expression of inflammatory genes. Additionally, licorice root tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the growth of acne-causing bacteria on the skin. By reducing inflammation, regulating sebum production, and inhibiting bacterial growth, licorice root tea can help to improve the overall health and appearance of oily skin. It's important to note that while licorice root tea can be a helpful addition to a skincare routine, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for severe or persistent skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: In a tea pot, add 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root and pour 1 cup of boiling water over it, let it steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the active compounds and cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root and discard it.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea and stir well to dissolve, honey has natural antibacterial properties and will help to reduce oiliness.
Optionally, you can add a squeeze of lemon juice for its vitamin C and astringent properties, this step is optional and should be based on personal preference.
Drink the tea, it is recommended to drink 1-2 cups of licorice root tea daily for best results, it may take several weeks to see improvements in skin oiliness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Marshmallow root tea helps with oily skin because it contains mucilage, a slippery substance that can help to remove excess oil from the skin. This mucilage forms a protective layer on the skin, which can help to prevent further oil production. Additionally, marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin. The tea can also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of pores and giving the skin a more matte finish. Finally, marshmallow root tea is a natural and gentle option for managing oily skin, making it a good choice for those who prefer to avoid harsh chemical treatments.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, bring 1 cup of water to a boil, then add 1 teaspoon of dried marshmallow root and let it simmer on low heat for 15 minutes.
After 15 minutes, remove the pot from heat and let the marshmallow root tea cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Strain the tea to remove the marshmallow root pieces and discard them.
Optionally, add a natural sweetener like honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the marshmallow root tea once or twice a day, as needed, to help soothe and balance oily skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tea helps with oily skin because it contains mucilage, a substance that has excellent skincare properties. Mucilage has the ability to absorb excess oil from the skin, making it an effective natural remedy for oily skin. Additionally, slippery elm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation caused by oily skin. Furthermore, it has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily skin. Lastly, slippery elm tea can help soothe and moisturize the skin, which can help regulate sebum production and improve the overall health and appearance of oily skin.
remedy_recipe: In a tea pot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried slippery elm bark with 8 ounces of water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
After steeping, strain the tea to remove any solid particles and let it cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Add a squeeze of lemon (to taste) to the tea for added skin-clearing benefits.
Drink the tea regularly, once or twice a day, for best results.
For external use, let the tea cool down completely and use it as a toner by applying it to your face with a cotton ball.

title: best herbal teas for oily skin
